rulings.Case Title: ESTHER JUAREZ et al VS. JORGE MARTIN DE ANDA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Approval Of The Distribution Of The Funds From The Sale Of The Property
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 11, 2011, Line 3, OTHER party KEVIN SINGER's Motion For Approval Of The Distribution Of The Funds From The Sale Of The Property. Granted in its entirety. Judge Kahn discloses that he believes that Amy Harrington is married to Judge Gerardo Sandoval, a judge of the San Francisco Superior Court, but that he is able to decide this motion fairly and impartially. Plaintiffs' objection to the fees of Mr. Ramos is overruled. It was reasonable for ms. Harrington to hire counsel and the fees of her counsel were reasonable. rulings.Case Title: DAVID YANG et al VS. FOLSOM/ FIRST LLC A LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant/Cross-Complainant The Metropolitan Association'S Motion For Summary Adjudication Of Causes Of Action In Its Amended Cross-Complaint For Declaratory Relief (Against Sd Deacon Of California, Inc.)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 11, 2011, Line 4, DEFENDANT/CROSS-COMPLAINANT THE METROPOLITAN ASSOCIATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF CAUSES OF ACTION IN ITS AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF (AGAINST SD DEACON OF CALIFORNIA, INC.) Granted as to the seventh cause of action for declaratory relief regarding the duty to defend and denied as to all other causes of action for which summary adjudication is sought. Regarding the seventh cause of action, the contract between the parties does not exclude the duty to defend and the claims asserted are embraced by the indemnity provisions in the contract. Regarding the eighth cause of action for declaratory relief regarding the duty to indemnify, the motion for summary adjudication fails because there has been no recovery against The Metropolitan Association. Regarding the fifth and sixth causes of action, the motion for summary adjudication fails because The Metropolitan Association has not established the amount of damages, which is a required element of its contract claims. A party may move for summary adjudication of less than an entire cause of action only in very limited circumstances, which are not met here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE L. VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel And Req. For Sanctions; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 11, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, RICHARD CURCI's Motion To Compel And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. Motion to compel responses to supplemental interrogatories (set one) and supplemental request for production (set one) granted. Motion to deem requests for admissions (set two) granted. Plaintiff shall pay sanctions of $875 to Defendant SFUSD. The responses shall be provided and the sanctions paid within ten days of notice of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANGOTTI & REILLY, INC. VS. RINCON RESIDENTIAL TOWERS, LLC
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion For Costs Of Enforcing Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 11, 2011, Line 8, PETITIONER ANGOTTI & REILLY, INC.'S Motion For Costs Of Enforcing Judgment. Granted in part. Petitioner is awarded fees of $200,000, which is approximately two-thirds of the revised amount sought in petitioner's supplemental papers. While petitioner is entitled to the recovery of fees and petitioner has made a reasonable good faith effort to supports its request with the without compromising its litigation positions, nonetheless a one-third reduction is appropriate due to the insufficiency of some of the information provided because of block-billing and redactions. rulings.Case Title: SIDEMAN & BANCROFT LLP, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 11, 2011, Line 15, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JOSE CISNEROS, TREASURE AND TAX COLLECTOR'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the second cause of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the first, third and fourth causes of action. The determination of the merits of the second cause of action requires further factual development and cannot be resolved at the pleading stage. The complaint and judicially noticeable materials do not show that Proposition Q violates Proposition 218, section 17041.5 or the single subject rule nor has plaintiff shown that it can allege sufficient facts to state cognizable claims on these matters. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: SHARTSIS FRIESE LLP VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 11, 2011, Line 18, DEFENDANTS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, JOSE CISNEROS, TREASURE AND TAX COLLECTOR'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the second cause of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the first, third and fourth causes of action. The determination of the merits of the second cause of action requires further factual development and cannot be resolved at the pleading stage. The complaint and judicially noticeable materials do not show that Proposition Q violates Proposition 218, section 17041.5 or the single subject rule nor has plaintiff shown that it can allege sufficient facts to state cognizable claims on these matters. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: KIRK B FREEMAN VS. FELICIA VALLERA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And For Sanctions;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, July 11, 2012, Line 7, PLAINTIFF KIRK FREEMAN's Motion To Compel Production Of Documents And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Mark Schreiber, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed. Motion to compel responses to request for production (set one) granted. Defendant Felicia Vallera shall pay sanctions of $790 to Plaintiff Kirk Freeman. The responses shall be provided and the sanctions shall be paid within ten days of notice of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to mschreiber@cwclaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
